import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

# Load all experiment data files
experiment_data_path_list = [
    "experiments_sonnet45/2026-05-18_06-11-49_sparse_routing_peft_attempt_0/logs/0-run/experiment_results/experiment_0bd6f2bbcc5e435084b2ebb4e58d260a_proc_71384/experiment_data.npy",
    "experiments_sonnet45/2026-05-18_06-11-49_sparse_routing_peft_attempt_0/logs/0-run/experiment_results/experiment_63c3f69eafff40aeb11d846b204a57cb_proc_71385/experiment_data.npy",
    "experiments_sonnet45/2026-05-18_06-11-49_sparse_routing_peft_attempt_0/logs/0-run/experiment_results/experiment_1cb1ac9cbd10451cb414a20660f01200_proc_71381/experiment_data.npy",
]

all_experiment_data = []
try:
    for experiment_data_path in experiment_data_path_list:
        experiment_data = np.load(
            os.path.join(os.getenv("AI_SCIENTIST_ROOT", ""), experiment_data_path),
            allow_pickle=True,
        ).item()
        all_experiment_data.append(experiment_data)
    print(f"Successfully loaded {len(all_experiment_data)} experiment data files")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")
    all_experiment_data = []


# Aggregate data by dataset
def aggregate_metrics(all_data, dataset_name, metric_path):
    """Extract and aggregate metrics across runs"""
    values = []
    for exp_data in all_data:
        if dataset_name in exp_data:
            keys = metric_path.split(".")
            data = exp_data[dataset_name]
            for key in keys:
                data = data[key]
            values.append(np.array(data))
    if len(values) == 0:
        return None, None
    min_len = min(len(v) for v in values)
    values = [v[:min_len] for v in values]
    values = np.array(values)
    mean = np.mean(values, axis=0)
    std_err = np.std(values, axis=0) / np.sqrt(len(values))
    return mean, std_err


# Plot aggregated training/validation curves for each dataset
for dataset_name in ["imdb", "agnews", "sst2"]:
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(18, 10))

        metrics_to_plot = [
            ("losses.train", "Training Loss", axes[0, 0], "b"),
            ("losses.val", "Validation Loss", axes[0, 1], "g"),
            ("losses.task", "Task Loss", axes[1, 0], "r"),
            ("metrics.sparsity", "Sparsity", axes[1, 1], "m"),
            ("metrics.eats", "EATS Score", axes[1, 2], "c"),
        ]

        for metric_path, title, ax, color in metrics_to_plot:
            mean, std_err = aggregate_metrics(
                all_experiment_data, dataset_name, metric_path
            )
            if mean is not None:
                epochs = list(range(1, len(mean) + 1))
                ax.plot(
                    epochs,
                    mean,
                    f"{color}-",
                    marker="o",
                    markevery=max(1, len(epochs) // 5),
                    label="Mean",
                )
                ax.fill_between(
                    epochs,
                    mean - std_err,
                    mean + std_err,
                    alpha=0.3,
                    color=color,
                    label="±SE",
                )
                ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
                ax.set_ylabel(title.split(" - ")[-1] if " - " in title else title)
                ax.set_title(f"{dataset_name.upper()} - {title}")
                ax.legend()
                ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

        # Special handling for accuracy plot
        train_acc_mean, train_acc_se = aggregate_metrics(
            all_experiment_data, dataset_name, "metrics.train_acc"
        )
        val_acc_mean, val_acc_se = aggregate_metrics(
            all_experiment_data, dataset_name, "metrics.val_acc"
        )
        if train_acc_mean is not None and val_acc_mean is not None:
            epochs = list(range(1, len(train_acc_mean) + 1))
            axes[0, 2].plot(
                epochs,
                train_acc_mean,
                "b-",
                marker="o",
                markevery=max(1, len(epochs) // 5),
                label="Train Mean",
            )
            axes[0, 2].fill_between(
                epochs,
                train_acc_mean - train_acc_se,
                train_acc_mean + train_acc_se,
                alpha=0.3,
                color="b",
            )
            axes[0, 2].plot(
                epochs,
                val_acc_mean,
                "g-",
                marker="s",
                markevery=max(1, len(epochs) // 5),
                label="Val Mean",
            )
            axes[0, 2].fill_between(
                epochs,
                val_acc_mean - val_acc_se,
                val_acc_mean + val_acc_se,
                alpha=0.3,
                color="g",
            )
            axes[0, 2].set_xlabel("Epoch")
            axes[0, 2].set_ylabel("Accuracy")
            axes[0, 2].set_title(f"{dataset_name.upper()} - Accuracy (Mean ± SE)")
            axes[0, 2].legend()
            axes[0, 2].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(

            ),
            dpi=150,

        )
        plt.close()
        print(f"Created aggregated training metrics plot for {dataset_name}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(
            f"Error creating aggregated training metrics plot for {dataset_name}: {e}"
        )
        plt.close()
